mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue acceleration, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index below 100 indicates severe manufacturing contraction that suppresses corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when INDPRO falls below 100, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels above 250 combined with a rising federal funds rate signal aggressive monetary tightening that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and FEDFUNDS rises, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3 percent confirms strong aggregate demand that boosts corporate profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent momentum where industrial production rises while the unemployment rate falls signals synchronized supply-side recovery and labor market tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
